intro: The clearest split is that Agora carries low lock-in and Dyte carries medium lock-in. Agora is Real-time audio, video, and interactive live streaming SDK with global SD-RTN network infrastructure; Dyte is Video call and audio room SDK with custom UI components, recording, and live streaming. The data points Agora at chat applications and live collaboration features, and Dyte at chat applications and live collaboration features. Pricing: Agora is freemium, free tier; Dyte is freemium from $0.00. Lock-in is low for Agora and medium for Dyte, with DX at 5/5 vs 5/5 and transparency at 4/5 vs 5/5. On listed strengths, Agora emphasises chat applications and live collaboration features, and Dyte emphasises chat applications and live collaboration features. Only Agora lists an enterprise tier in the data. Honest trade-off: neither fits static projects, so if that is your context, look elsewhere; otherwise pick on the pivotal factor above and the DX score that matters most to your team.
quick_take: Agora is for chat applications; Dyte is for chat applications; decide based on how much lock-in you tolerate.
verdict_a: Choose Agora if you prefer low lock-in and easier exit and your workload matches chat applications. Skip it if your context is static projects.
verdict_b: Choose Dyte if you can accept medium lock-in in exchange for scope and your workload matches chat applications. Skip it if your context is static projects.

faq.a: Entry pricing is listed as unknown for Agora and $0.00 for Dyte. Both offer a free tier for getting started. Total cost at scale depends on usage and plan details that are not in this dataset, so confirm with each vendor.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Agora to Dyte?
faq.a: Agora is tagged with low lock-in (proprietary sd-rtn network and signaling; switching requires rewrite). Dyte is tagged with medium lock-in (sdk tied but data portable). Plan for SDK replacement, data export, and a parallel-run period; the dataset does not include migration tooling details.

faq.q: Which has better developer experience, Agora or Dyte?
faq.a: Both are rated 5/5 for developer experience in this dataset. With equal scores, the choice comes down to the specific SDK surface and docs you will use day to day. Run a short spike on each before deciding.

faq.q: Is Dyte a good alternative to Agora?
faq.a: Yes for teams whose workload matches chat applications, since both list the same fit. Dyte is flagged as not for static projects, so confirm your context does not fall there.
